creator: cmt@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Joel Bodenmann from comment #4)
The actual problem ist that kicad uses dynamic_cast for downcasting across translation units and shared objects (a lot of kicad's code is loaded dynamically). That is a "generally bad idea" on clang/libc++ platforms (as ours) but "kinda works most times" on gcc/libstdc++ platforms (as almost all Linuxen are) (I have no idea what the msvc folks do, and I also can't say anything about the OSX platform and their toolchain).
The problem started when kicad "optimized" some things - including the settings manager - in 7.0.3 (we had earlier fallout from that in other places, where it was workaroundable, but those workarounds are not feasible in the settings manager), and it hasn't changed up to and including 7.0.7 (which is to say, forget about 7.0.6, unless you want to learn about the "implementation dependent" parts of the C++ runtime and ELF binary format).
